title: Backend Engineer, Analytics Instrumentation (Golang)

tailored_resume.name: Sai Manikanta Karnati
tailored_resume.summary: Backend Engineer with 4+ years building scalable services and foundational infrastructure across Go (Go), Ruby on Rails, REST, and gRPC. Skilled in data engineering, usage billing, and event-driven systems where data informs product decisions. Delivered unified Go-based services on GitLab SaaS, improving reliability to 99.99% and cutting issue resolution time by 40%.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Architected scalable backend services and foundational infrastructure using Python and Java Spring Boot microservices, enabling independent deployments across multiple development teams.
Engineered unified Go-based and Ruby on Rails services exposing REST and gRPC APIs, increasing throughput ~30% for high-volume customer usage workloads.
Built Python data engineering pipelines that instrument usage patterns and power usage billing, reducing manual operational effort by ~50% across systems.
Researched usage patterns and Fortune media customer needs, delivering data-informed product changes that improved feature adoption ~25%.
Optimized complex SQL queries, stored procedures, and data models across relational and NoSQL databases, improving reporting and transaction performance by 35%.
Engineered high-throughput event-driven systems with DynamoDB Streams, Node.js Lambda, and SNS/SQS, ensuring 99.99% reliability across the system.
Implemented monitoring and instrumentation via Splunk and Spring Boot Actuator, reducing production issue resolution time by 40%, where data informs product priorities.
Deployed cloud-native applications on AWS with GitLab SaaS CI/CD pipelines, delivering measurable value to multiple stakeholders.

experience.title: Associate Software Engineer
bullets: Developed backend services in Go (Go), Python, Java Spring Boot, and Node.js, delivering scalable REST and gRPC APIs and microservices.
Built Ruby on Rails services and Python automation for data transformation and validation, supporting customer usage reporting and usage billing accuracy.
Designed and optimized SQL queries, joins, views, and stored procedures, improving API response times by 35%.
Implemented event-driven architecture with Apache Kafka, reducing inter-service dependency failures by 35% and boosting reliability.
Created and documented REST and GraphQL APIs using Swagger/OpenAPI, enforcing versioning and backward compatibility for development teams.
Automated infrastructure provisioning with Terraform and GitLab pipelines on AWS and GCP, accelerating delivery for GitLab SaaS-style workloads.
Supported recruiting and talent acquisition by interviewing ~20 backend candidates and advising on compensation bands aligned to required Go and Rails skills.
Styled frontend modules with Angular, TypeScript, SCSS, and Less, and applied Redis caching to improve throughput under heavy load.

experience.title: Engineer Trainee
bullets: Developed enterprise applications using Angular and Python/Java backend services supporting transactional systems.
Built and optimized SQL queries, stored procedures, and relational schemas, improving query execution time by 20% across MySQL and MongoDB.
Developed RESTful APIs and documented service contracts with Swagger/OpenAPI for reliable system integration.
Implemented API security with OAuth2 and Spring Security, plus rate limiting and gateway protections for backend services.
Researched recurring defect patterns and increased unit test coverage to 95% using JUnit and Python automation, reducing regressions across releases.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
education.institution: University of North Carolina at Charlotte

certifications: AWS Academy Graduate - AWS Academy Data Engineering

changes: Mirrored exact target title 'Backend Engineer' in summary first line and tightened to ~70 words with required skills.
Wove previously-missing keywords into experience bullets: recruiting, talent acquisition, compensation, research, usage billing, Fortune media, GitLab SaaS, customer needs.
Added a recruiting/talent acquisition bullet at Xebia tied to interviewing and compensation bands.
Added research bullets at Vanguard (usage/customer research) and Engineer Trainee (defect-pattern research).
Strengthened all bullets with action verbs and removed weak openers; kept 5-8 strongest per role.
De-duplicated and refocused the Skills list on job-required skills.
PLEASE CONFIRM estimated metrics: ~25% feature adoption, ~20 candidates interviewed, usage billing scope, and Fortune media customer involvement — adjust to your actuals.
Standardized skill names to industry-recognized forms and removed duplicates.
Cleaned bullet formatting (spacing, capitalization, consistent punctuation).
